code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    subject_map = {}
    for triple in triples:
        if triple.subject not in subject_map:
            subject_map[triple.subject] = []
        subject_map[triple.subject].append((triple.predicate, triple.object))

    sentence = ""
    predicates = {
        "cityServed": lambda obj: f"serves the city of {obj}",
        "location": lambda obj: f"is located in {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda obj: f"has an elevation of {obj} metres above sea level",
        "runwayLength": lambda obj: f"has a runway length of {obj} metres",
        "country": lambda obj: f"is located in {obj}",
        "capital": lambda obj: f", where the capital is {obj}",
        "isPartOf": lambda obj: f", which is part of {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda obj: f"has an official language of {obj}",
        "mayor": lambda obj: f"has a mayor of {obj}",
        "operatingOrganisation": lambda obj: f"is operated by {obj}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": lambda obj: f"has a first runway length of {obj} feet",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": lambda obj: f"has a first runway surface type of {obj}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": lambda obj: f"has a third runway length of {obj} feet",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": lambda obj: f"has an ICAO location identifier of {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": lambda obj: f"has a location identifier of {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": lambda obj: f"has an elevation of {obj} feet above sea level",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": lambda obj: f"has an IATA location identifier of {obj}",
        "nativeName": lambda obj: f"has a native name of {obj}",
        "leaderParty": lambda obj: f"has a leader party of {obj}",
        "class": lambda obj: f"which belongs to the class of {obj}",
        "division": lambda obj: f"which belongs to the division of {obj}",
        "order": lambda obj: f"which belongs to the order of {obj}",
    }
    for subject, triples_list in subject_map.items():
        if sentence:
            sentence += " which "
        sentence += f"{subject} "
        for i, (predicate, obj) in enumerate(triples_list):
            if predicate in predicates:
                sentence += predicates[predicate](obj)
            else:
                sentence += f", with a {predicate} of {obj}"
            if i < len(triples_list) - 1:
                sentence += ", "
        if len(subject_map) > 1:
            sentence += ", "
    return sentence + "."

You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: Airport. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)

"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    subject_map = {}
    for triple in triples:
        if triple.subject not in subject_map:
            subject_map[triple.subject] = []
        subject_map[triple.subject].append((triple.predicate, triple.object))

    sentence = ""
    predicates = {
        "cityServed": lambda obj: f"serves the city of {obj}",
        "location": lambda obj: f"is located in {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda obj: f"has an elevation of {obj} metres above sea level",
        "runwayLength": lambda obj: f"has a runway length of {obj} metres",
        "country": lambda obj: f"is located in {obj}",
        "capital": lambda obj: f", where the capital is {obj}",
        "isPartOf": lambda obj: f", which is part of {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda obj: f"has an official language of {obj}",
        "mayor": lambda obj: f"has a mayor of {obj}",
        "operatingOrganisation": lambda obj: f"is operated by {obj}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": lambda obj: f"has a first runway length of {obj} feet",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": lambda obj: f"has a first runway surface type of {obj}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": lambda obj: f"has a third runway length of {obj} feet",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": lambda obj: f"has an ICAO location identifier of {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": lambda obj: f"has a location identifier of {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": lambda obj: f"has an elevation of {obj} feet above sea level",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": lambda obj: f"has an IATA location identifier of {obj}",
        "nativeName": lambda obj: f"has a native name of {obj}",
        "leaderParty": lambda obj: f"has a leader party of {obj}",
    }
    for subject, triples_list in subject_map.items():
        if sentence:
            sentence += " which "
        sentence += f"{subject} "
        for i, (predicate, obj) in enumerate(triples_list):
            if predicate in predicates:
                sentence += predicates[predicate](obj)
            else:
                sentence += f", with a {predicate} of {obj}"
            if i < len(triples_list) - 1:
                sentence += ", "
        if len(subject_map) > 1:
            sentence += ", "
    return sentence + "."
```

### Inspiration 1 (Score: 0.3402)
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    subject_map = {}
    object_map = {}
    for triple in triples:
        if triple.subject not in subject_map:
            subject_map[triple.subject] = []
        subject_map[triple.subject].append((triple.predicate, triple.object))

        if triple.object not in object_map:
            object_map[triple.object] = []
        object_map[triple.object].append((triple.predicate, triple.subject))

    sentence = ""
    main_subjects = []
    for subject, triples in subject_map.items():
        if subject not in main_subjects:
            main_subjects.append(subject)
            if sentence:
                sentence += " and "
            sentence += f"{subject} "
            for i, (predicate, obj) in enumerate(triples):
                if predicate == "cityServed":
                    sentence += f"serves the city of {obj}"
                elif predicate == "mayor":
                    sentence += f"which has a mayor of {obj}"
                elif predicate == "isPartOf":
                    sentence += f"which is part of {obj}"
                elif predicate == "operatingOrganisation":
                    sentence += f"and is operated by {obj}"
                elif predicate == "location":
                    sentence += f"located in {obj}"
                elif predicate == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel":
                    sentence += f"with an elevation of {obj} metres above sea level"
                elif predicate == "runwayLength":
                    sentence += f"and a runway length of {obj} metres"
                elif predicate == "country":
                    sentence += f"which is located in {obj}"
                elif predicate == "capital":
                    sentence += f"where the capital is {obj}"
                elif predicate == "language":
                    sentence += f"with a language of {obj}"
                elif predicate == "leader":
                    sentence += f"and has a leader of {obj}"
                elif predicate == "owner":
                    sentence += f"which is owned by {obj}"
                elif predicate == "1stRunwayLengthFeet":
                    sentence += f"with a first runway length of {obj} feet"
                elif predicate == "1stRunwaySurfaceType":
                    sentence += f"and a first runway surface type of {obj}"
                elif predicate == "3rdRunwayLengthFeet":
                    sentence += f"and a third runway length of {obj} feet"
                elif predicate == "icaoLocationIdentifier":
                    sentence += f"with an ICAO location identifier of {obj}"
                elif predicate == "locationIdentifier":
                    sentence += f"and a location identifier of {obj}"
                elif predicate == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet":
                    sentence += f"with an elevation of {obj} feet above sea level"
                elif predicate == "iataLocationIdentifier":
                    sentence += f"with an IATA location identifier of {obj}"
                elif predicate == "nativeName":
                    sentence += f"and a native name of {obj}"
                elif predicate == "leaderParty":
                    sentence += f"with a leader party of {obj}"
                else:
                    sentence += f"and a {predicate} of {obj}"
                if i < len(triples) - 1:
                    sentence += ", "

    for obj, triples in object_map.items():
        if obj in sentence:
            for i, (predicate, subject) in enumerate(triples):
                if subject not in main_subjects:
                    sentence += f", {predicate} of {subject}"

    return sentence + "."
```

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    subject_map = {}
    for triple in triples:
        if triple.subject not in subject_map:
            subject_map[triple.subject] = []
        subject_map[triple.subject].append((triple.predicate, triple.object))

    sentence = ""
    predicates = {
        "cityServed": lambda obj: f"serves the city of {obj}",
        "location": lambda obj: f"is located in {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda obj: f"has an elevation of {obj} metres above sea level",
        "runwayLength": lambda obj: f"has a runway length of {obj} metres",
        "country": lambda obj: f"is located in {obj}",
        "capital": lambda obj: f", where the capital is {obj}",
        "isPartOf": lambda obj: f", which is part of {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda obj: f"has an official language of {obj}",
        "mayor": lambda obj: f"has a mayor of {obj}",
        "operatingOrganisation": lambda obj: f"is operated by {obj}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": lambda obj: f"has a first runway length of {obj} feet",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": lambda obj: f"has a first runway surface type of {obj}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": lambda obj: f"has a third runway length of {obj} feet",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": lambda obj: f"has an ICAO location identifier of {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": lambda obj: f"has a location identifier of {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": lambda obj: f"has an elevation of {obj} feet above sea level",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": lambda obj: f"has an IATA location identifier of {obj}",
        "nativeName": lambda obj: f"has a native name of {obj}",
        "leaderParty": lambda obj: f"has a leader party of {obj}",
    }
    for subject, triples_list in subject_map.items():
        if sentence:
            sentence += " which "
        sentence += f"{subject} "
        for i, (predicate, obj) in enumerate(triples_list):
            if predicate in predicates:
                sentence += predicates[predicate](obj)
            else:
                sentence += f", with a {predicate} of {obj}"
            if i < len(triples_list) - 1:
                sentence += ", "
        if len(subject_map) > 1:
            sentence += ", "
    return sentence + "."

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    subject_map = {}
    for triple in triples:
        if triple.subject not in subject_map:
            subject_map[triple.subject] = []
        subject_map[triple.subject].append((triple.predicate, triple.object))

    sentence = ""
    predicates = {
        "cityServed": lambda obj: f"serves the city of {obj}",
        "location": lambda obj: f"is located in {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda obj: f"has an elevation of {obj} metres above sea level",
        "runwayLength": lambda obj: f"has a runway length of {obj} metres",
        "country": lambda obj: f"is located in {obj}",
        "capital": lambda obj: f", where the capital is {obj}",
        "isPartOf": lambda obj: f", which is part of {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda obj: f"has an official language of {obj}",
        "mayor": lambda obj: f"has a mayor of {obj}",
        "operatingOrganisation": lambda obj: f"is operated by {obj}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": lambda obj: f"has a first runway length of {obj} feet",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": lambda obj: f"has a first runway surface type of {obj}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": lambda obj: f"has a third runway length of {obj} feet",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": lambda obj: f"has an ICAO location identifier of {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": lambda obj: f"has a location identifier of {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": lambda obj: f"has an elevation of {obj} feet above sea level",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": lambda obj: f"has an IATA location identifier of {obj}",
        "nativeName": lambda obj: f"has a native name of {obj}",
        "leaderParty": lambda obj: f"has a leader party of {obj}",
        "class": lambda obj: f"which belongs to the class of {obj}",
        "division": lambda obj: f"which belongs to the division of {obj}",
        "order": lambda obj: f"which belongs to the order of {obj}",
    }
    subjects = list(subject_map.keys())
    if len(subjects) == 1:
        subject = subjects[0]
        sentence += f"{subject} "
        for i, (predicate, obj) in enumerate(subject_map[subject]):
            if predicate in predicates:
                sentence += predicates
